requirements.requirement: Subpart M applies to all construction workplaces where workers are exposed to fall hazards
Competent Person is defined as one who is capable of identifying existing and predictable hazards and who has authorization to take prompt corrective measures
Walking/working surface means any surface, whether horizontal or vertical, on which an employee walks or works

Each employee on a walking/working surface with an unprotected side or edge which is 6 feet or more above a lower level shall be protected by the use of guardrail systems, safety net systems, or personal fall arrest systems
common_violations: Using a height threshold other than 6 feet (e.g., 10 feet)
Not specifying all three acceptable protection methods
Failing to require protection at unprotected sides AND edges

requirements.requirement: Each employee on a walking/working surface shall be protected from falling through holes more than 6 feet above a lower level by personal fall arrest systems, covers, or guardrail systems
common_violations: Not addressing floor holes and openings separately from edges
Missing cover requirements for holes

requirements.requirement: Each employee on a walking/working surface shall be protected from objects falling through holes by covers
common_violations: Addressing only fall-into hazards but not fall-through-hole hazards

requirements.requirement: Each employee on ramps, runways, and other walkways shall be protected from falling 6 feet or more by guardrail systems
common_violations: Allowing personal fall arrest on ramps instead of guardrails
Not addressing ramps and runways specifically

requirements.requirement: Each employee working on a low-slope roof with unprotected sides and edges 6 feet or more above lower levels shall be protected by guardrail systems, safety net systems, personal fall arrest systems, or a combination of warning line system and guardrail/safety net/personal fall arrest/safety monitoring system
common_violations: Not specifying warning line system option for low-slope roofs
Using only a safety monitor without a warning line on low-slope roofs

requirements.requirement: Each employee on a steep roof with unprotected sides and edges 6 feet or more above lower levels shall be protected by guardrail systems with toeboards, safety net systems, or personal fall arrest systems
common_violations: Not differentiating between steep and low-slope roof requirements
Missing toeboard requirement for guardrails on steep roofs

requirements.requirement: Each employee engaged in residential construction activities 6 feet or more above lower levels shall be protected by guardrail systems, safety net systems, or personal fall arrest systems unless employer can demonstrate alternative plan is equivalent
common_violations: Claiming residential construction exemption without documented alternative plan

requirements.requirement: Each employee in a hoist area shall be protected from falling 6 feet or more by guardrail systems or personal fall arrest systems. If guardrail is removed for hoisting, employee must be protected by personal fall arrest system
common_violations: Not addressing temporary guardrail removal during hoisting operations

1926.502.title: Fall Protection Systems Criteria and Practices

requirements.requirement: Top edge of guardrail systems shall be 42 inches plus or minus 3 inches above the walking/working surface
common_violations: Specifying incorrect guardrail height (e.g., 36 inches or 48 inches)
Not specifying the ±3 inch tolerance
Using a single height without acceptable range

requirements.requirement: Midrails shall be installed at a height midway between the top edge of the guardrail system and the walking/working surface
requirements.midrail_position: midway between top rail and surface
common_violations: Not specifying midrail requirement
Incorrect midrail height specification

requirements.requirement: Top rail of guardrail system must withstand a force of at least 200 pounds applied in any outward or downward direction at any point along the top edge
common_violations: Not specifying load-bearing capacity of guardrails
Specifying incorrect force requirement
Missing directional force specification

requirements.requirement: Midrails, screens, mesh, intermediate vertical members, or equivalent intermediate structural members must withstand a force of at least 150 pounds applied in any downward or outward direction at any point
common_violations: Not specifying midrail load capacity
Confusing midrail and top rail force requirements

requirements.requirement: If wire rope is used for top rails, it must be flagged at not more than 6-foot intervals with high-visibility material
common_violations: Not addressing wire rope guardrail visibility requirements

requirements.requirement: When guardrail systems are used at holes, they shall be erected on all unprotected sides or edges of the hole
common_violations: Not specifying guardrail coverage on all sides of holes

requirements.requirement: Guardrail systems shall be surfaced to prevent injury from punctures, lacerations, or snagging of clothing
common_violations: Not specifying surface condition requirements for guardrails

requirements.requirement: The ends of top rails and midrails shall not overhang the terminal posts except where such overhang does not constitute a projection hazard
common_violations: Not addressing guardrail end conditions

requirements.requirement: Safety nets shall be installed as close as practicable under the walking/working surface but not more than 30 feet below
common_violations: Not specifying maximum distance for safety net installation
Incorrect maximum distance specification

requirements.requirement: Safety nets shall extend outward from the outermost projection of the work surface as follows: up to 5 feet - minimum 8 feet outward; 5-10 feet - minimum 10 feet outward; more than 10 feet - minimum 13 feet outward
common_violations: Not specifying net extension distances
Using a single extension distance for all heights

requirements.requirement: Personal fall arrest systems shall limit maximum arresting force on an employee to 1,800 pounds when used with a body harness
common_violations: Not specifying maximum arresting force
Allowing body belts for fall arrest (prohibited since 1998)

requirements.requirement: Personal fall arrest systems shall be rigged so that an employee can neither free fall more than 6 feet nor contact any lower level
common_violations: Not specifying maximum free fall distance
Incorrect free fall distance specification

requirements.requirement: Personal fall arrest systems shall bring an employee to a complete stop and limit maximum deceleration distance to 3.5 feet
common_violations: Not specifying deceleration distance limits
Incorrect deceleration distance

requirements.requirement: Personal fall arrest systems shall be inspected prior to each use for wear, damage, and other deterioration, and defective components shall be removed from service
common_violations: Not requiring pre-use inspection
Not specifying removal of defective equipment from service

requirements.requirement: Anchorages used for attachment of personal fall arrest equipment shall be independent of any anchorage being used to support or suspend platforms and shall be capable of supporting at least 5,000 pounds per employee attached
common_violations: Not specifying anchorage strength requirement
Incorrect anchorage strength (e.g., 3,000 pounds)
Not requiring independent anchorage from platform supports
Not specifying per-employee capacity

requirements.requirement: Personal fall arrest systems and components subjected to impact loading shall be immediately removed from service and not used again until inspected and determined to be undamaged and suitable for reuse
common_violations: Not requiring immediate removal after impact loading
Not specifying post-fall inspection requirement

requirements.requirement: The employer shall provide for prompt rescue of employees in the event of a fall or shall assure the self-rescue capability of employees
common_violations: No rescue plan documented
Not addressing suspension trauma
Not specifying rescue procedures or self-rescue capability

requirements.requirement: Body belts are not acceptable as part of a personal fall arrest system. Body belts may only be used in positioning device systems
common_violations: Allowing body belts for fall arrest
Not specifying full body harness requirement
Not distinguishing between fall arrest and positioning systems

requirements.requirement: Warning line systems shall be erected around all sides of the roof work area not less than 6 feet from the roof edge
common_violations: Incorrect warning line distance from edge
Not erecting warning lines on all sides

requirements.requirement: Safety monitoring systems shall be used only on low-slope roofs and only in conjunction with warning line systems (except for roofing work on low-slope roofs)
common_violations: Using safety monitoring as sole protection on non-roofing work
Not pairing safety monitoring with warning line systems

requirements.requirement: Toeboards shall be at least 3.5 inches tall from the top edge of the toeboard to the walking/working surface and shall withstand a force of at least 50 pounds applied in any downward or outward direction
common_violations: Not specifying toeboard height requirement
Missing toeboard force resistance specification

requirements.requirement: The employer shall provide a training program for each employee who might be exposed to fall hazards. The program shall enable each employee to recognize the hazards of falling and the procedures to be followed to minimize these hazards
common_violations: No documented training program
Training not covering hazard recognition
Not training ALL exposed employees

requirements.requirement: The employer shall assure that each employee has been trained by a competent person qualified in: the nature of fall hazards in the work area, correct procedures for erecting/maintaining/disassembling/inspecting fall protection systems, use and operation of guardrail systems, personal fall arrest systems, safety net systems, warning line systems, safety monitoring systems, controlled access zones, and other protection used, role of each employee in the safety monitoring system, limitations on the use of mechanical equipment during roofing work, correct procedures for equipment and materials handling, and the role of employees in fall protection plans
required_training_topics: Nature of fall hazards in the work area
Correct procedures for erecting, maintaining, disassembling, and inspecting fall protection systems
Use and operation of fall protection systems
Role of employees in safety monitoring systems
Limitations on mechanical equipment during roofing work
Correct procedures for equipment and materials handling
Role of employees in fall protection plans
common_violations: Training not conducted by a competent person
Not covering all required training topics
Missing specific topics from the training curriculum

requirements.requirement: The employer shall verify compliance with training requirements by preparing a written certification record. The certification shall contain the name of the employee trained, the date of training, and the signature of the competent person who conducted the training
common_violations: No written training certification
Missing employee names on records
Missing training dates
Missing trainer signature
No verification of competent person status of trainer

requirements.requirement: When the employer has reason to believe that any affected employee who has already been trained does not have the understanding and skill required, the employer shall retrain each such employee. Circumstances requiring retraining include: changes in the workplace rendering previous training obsolete, changes in fall protection systems or equipment, and inadequacies in an affected employee's knowledge or use of fall protection systems
retraining_triggers: Changes in workplace rendering previous training obsolete
Changes in fall protection systems or equipment
Inadequacies in employee knowledge or use of fall protection
common_violations: No retraining policy documented
Not identifying specific retraining triggers
No process for evaluating employee competency after training
